title: 0-1 Product Build Loop
use_when: Use as the main Productize entry point when a founder, product leader, PM, or AI builder wants to go from raw idea, messy opportunity, or new product bet to a working first product slice through capability scoping, app setup, eval design, eval runs, failure analysis, and fixes.
do_not_use_when: Do not use 0-1 Product Build Loop for isolated PRD drafting, standalone growth diagnosis, finance valuation, stakeholder narrative, or post-launch metric readout when the user is not trying to create or materially reshape a product capability.
output_artifact: 0-1 product build brief with capability scope, curated data, app setup, eval matrix, behavior analysis, fixes, ship gate, and next learning loop
failure_modes: Treats 0-1 as a linear idea-to-PRD handoff instead of running the capability, application, eval, behavior-analysis, and fix loop until the product slice is demonstrably useful.
Scopes a product idea without defining the first testable capability, the curated data or evidence needed, the eval matrix, and the ship or repeat threshold.
Hands off to implementation before app setup, eval design, instrumentation, test scenarios, and verification criteria are clear enough for an AI coding agent to build safely.

examples.prompt: Use $0-1 to turn this raw AI workflow idea into the first product slice: scope the capability, curate the data, set up the app, design evals, run them, analyze failures, apply fixes, and decide whether to ship or repeat.
examples.expected_artifact: 0-1 product build brief with capability scope, curated data, app setup, eval matrix, behavior analysis, fixes, ship gate, and next learning loop

framework_fit: 0-1 Product Build Loop fits new-product work when the user needs the product, app, eval, and fix loop managed together rather than a linear strategy-to-handoff process. name: pmf-review

Holds the decision in indefinite analysis instead of forcing PMF, signal-but-not-PMF, no-PMF, or too-early — and naming the next route.

examples.prompt: Use $pmf-review on our last quarter cohort to decide whether we move to $grow or run another $0-1 lap.

framework_fit: PMF Review fits the gate between $0-1 and $grow. It pressure-tests demand-side evidence (retention, super-user pull, qualitative signal) against the original beachhead and forces a route decision before growth effort is committed.
